data.title: Manila Electric Cut to Hold From Add by SB Equities >MER.PH
Singapore Post May Be Profitable in FY 2026; Asset Monetization in Focus — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore Post may be profitable in FY 2026, but the focus is in monetization of its assets, Maybank Research's Jarick Seet says in a research report. Management will likely quicken its efforts to further reduce the size and scale of its loss-making postal network and sell some properties to unlock value for shareholders, the analyst says. Also, the company will probably engage with the Singapore authorities more actively to pursue the sale of SingPost Centre. The postal and e-commerce logistics services provider posted an underlying net loss of S$500,000 in 2H FY 2025, notes the brokerage, which trims the target price to S$0.74 from S$0.77 while maintaining the buy rating. Shares are 2.7% higher at S$0.575. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Pension SuperFund Withdraws De La Rue Offer Plan
data.description:
By Ian Walker

Pension SuperFund Capital said late Thursday that it doesn't plan to proceed with a formal offer to buy British software firm and bank note printer De La Rue.

Pension SuperFund approached De La Rue over a possible deal in January and the two companies have been in talks since.

De La Rue subsequently agreed to a 263 million-pound ($349.9 million) takeover by U.S. buyout firm Atlas Holdings on April 15, signaling the end of its nearly 80-year listing on the London Stock Exchange.

Under the Atlas deal, accepting shareholders will get 130 pence in cash for each De La Rue share, which was a 16% premium to its closing price of 112 pence on April 14.

De La Rue shares closed at 128.50 pence on Thursday.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0515 GMT - Singapore Post may be profitable in FY 2026, but the focus is in monetization of its assets, Maybank Research's Jarick Seet says in a research report. Management will likely quicken its efforts to further reduce the size and scale of its loss-making postal network and sell some properties to unlock value for shareholders, the analyst says. Also, the company will probably engage with the Singapore authorities more actively to pursue the sale of SingPost Centre. The postal and e-commerce logistics services provider posted an underlying net loss of S$500,000 in 2H FY 2025, notes the brokerage, which trims the target price to S$0.74 from S$0.77 while maintaining the buy rating. Shares are 2.7% higher at S$0.575. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0511 GMT - Singapore Airlines' passenger yields appears to be holding up, Citi Research analysts say in a note. This provided a boost to its full-year core earnings which slightly beat Citi's estimates. Incremental de-escalation in tariff tensions will likely help passenger pricing improve, and the relatively high payout ratio will likely support the share price, they add. The high payout could indicate delays in spending given on-going supply chain tightness, or higher confidence that Air India will not require further injections in the short term, they add. A key risk would be softer-than-expected passenger demand, ticket and cargo pricing, they add. Citi retains a neutral rating with a target price of S$6.34. Shares are 0.4% lower at S$6.85. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Dovish BOJ Board Member Calls for Pause in Rate Hikes on Tariff Concerns
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

Bank of Japan policy board member Toyoaki Nakamura said the central bank should pause monetary tightening, as higher tariffs could hurt the nation's virtuous cycle of income, spending and inflation.

"As the impact of U.S. tariff policies is currently a widely shared concern, it is necessary to carefully gauge developments in corporate profits, business fixed investment, wage hikes and other factors," Nakamura said in a speech Friday at an event held in Japan's southern prefecture of Fukuoka. "I believe it is appropriate for the bank to maintain its current monetary policy for the time being."

The BOJ left its policy rate at 0.5% earlier this month as it slashed its growth forecasts amid uncertainty about U.S. trade policy. Gov. Kazuo Ueda said underlying inflation may reach its target of 2% later than expected.

Nakamura, a former executive at Japanese electronics conglomerate Hitachi, said rushing to raise interest rates during an economic slowdown would damp consumption and investment. He also said that U.S. tariffs could weaken momentum for wage growth, which has finally gathered pace in recent years.

Government data released Friday showed that the Japanese economy shrank in the January-March period for the first time in a year. Economists say slowing exports and sluggish domestic demand could push the nation into a technical recession this quarter.

Despite risks surrounding the economic outlook, Ueda has said that the bank will continue to seek further interest-rate hikes if the economy and prices move in line with its projections.

Nakamura is considered among the most dovish members of the BOJ policy board. He has voted against the bank's decisions to raise rates.

His five-year term as a BOJ board member is set to end in June. The Japanese government has nominated former Mitsubishi Corp. executive Kazuyuki Masu to replace Nakamura.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: OTL - Odfjell Technology Ltd reports for the First Quarter of 2025
data.description:
Odfjell Technology Ltd is pleased to report its financial and operationalresults for the three-month period ended 31st March 2025.

Q1 Highlights:

- Revenue of NOK 1,330m - EBITDA of NOK 193m - EBIT of NOK 122m - Net profit of NOK 79m - Leverage ratio of 0.9X - Available liquidity of NOK 938m - Backlog of NOK 13.1bn - Q1 dividend of NOK 60m

For the full results, please see the attached report and presentation.

Key updates - Equity investment for 10% stake in Reelwell AS - Expansion of Powered Wire Drill Pipe contract with Vår Energi - Extension of platform drilling contract with ConocoPhillips - Successful start-up of operations on Visund and Brage - New contract signed for Tubular Running Services with ConcoPhillips - BSP Brunei mobile workover contract terminated

Simen Lieungh, CEO of Odfjell Technology AS, commented:

"Activity levels and operational performance remained stable across allsegmentsin the first quarter, with margin improvements compared to Q4 2024. ReportedEBITDA was NOK 193 million, after NOK 4 million in one-off and restructuringcosts related to our Performance Improvement Program.

Our operations are primarily in stable markets with resilient activity levels,supported by a strong link to oil and gas production - an area typically lessexposed to industry cyclicality. Combined with our solid contract portfolioandongoing performance initiatives, we are well positioned to deliver strongerearnings in the second half of 2025.

Cash flow was impacted by elevated capital expenditures and seasonal workingcapital build-up. We expect both capex and working capital levels to declineinthe second half of the year, supporting improved cash generation.

The cash dividend of NOK 60 million (NOK 1.52 per share) declared thisquarter,maintains the level from the two previous quarters. At the current shareprice,this represents a direct annualised yield of approximately 14%."

Chief Executive Officer Simen Lieungh, Chief Financial Officer Jone Torstensenand SVP Finance & Investor Relations Gert Haugland, of Odfjell Technology ASwill be hosting a webcast Friday 16th May 2025 to present the results, as wellas providing an outlook for the Company, at 09:30am UK time / 10:30am NorwayTime.

Stakeholders can either use the following link for the webcast or listen viatelephone using the details below.

Information on how to submit questions will be given at the beginning of theQ&Asession.

Please join the event 5-10 minutes prior to scheduled start time.

Webcast details: https://brrmedia.news/ND4_Q1_25 Conference call details:

or

data.title: Aegon plans share buyback as capital generation misses estimates in Q1
data.description:
Aegon said on Friday it planned to run a new 200 million euro ($224 million) share buyback, after the Dutch insurer posted lower than expected quarterly capital generation due to unfavourable mortality in its main U.S. market.

Its operating capital generation rose slightly to 267 million euros in the first quarter, but missed the median consensus of 275 million euros expected by analysts polled by the company.

($1 = 0.8922 euros)

data.title: Himalaya Food Gets Board Nod to Raise Up To INR400 Million Via Rights Issue
data.description:
Himalaya Food International Ltd has received board approval to raise up to 400 million Indian rupees through a rights issue, according to a Thursday filing with the Indian stock exchanges.

The company plans to issue 28,936,442 right equity shares of face value of 10 rupees each at a premium of 3.80 rupees each.

The rights issue is scheduled to open on June 3 and close on June 18.

data.title: Price pullback spurs gold demand in top Asian hubs
data.description:
Key points:
  • India's discount at $34 an ounce this week
  • Premiums of $0.25-$0.50/oz charged in Japan
  • Retail investment demand will likely be strong, ANZ says

Physical gold demand improved across most key Asian hubs this week as a pullback in global prices sparked buying interest among consumers.

This week, Indian dealers were offering a discount (XAU-IN-PREM) of up to $34 an ounce over official domestic prices – inclusive of 6% import and 3% sales levies - compared to last week's discount of up to $16.

"Demand was better this week compared to last, thanks to the price drop. But a lot of buyers are still waiting it out, hoping prices dip even more," said a Kolkata-based bullion dealer.

Domestic gold prices (MAUc1) were trading around 92,900 rupees per 10 grams on Friday after hitting a record high of 99,358 rupees last month.

"Prices need to settle down a bit. If gold drops another 2,000 rupees and hovers around 90,000 rupees, we could see demand really take off," said a Mumbai-based bullion dealer with a private bank.

Spot gold trading around $3,216.17 as of 0434 GMT on Friday. It hit an over one-month low of $3,120.14 on Thursday and is set for its biggest weekly decline since November.

Dealers in top gold consumer China charged premiums of $9-$50 an ounce over the global benchmark spot price, compared with premiums of $42-$49 last week. (XAU-CN-PREM)

"The correction in gold prices has for sure encouraged some bargain hunting and a good entry point for those who may have missed the move higher," said Ross Norman, an independent analyst.

"We expect jewellery demand to stabilise over the coming quarters, retail investment demand will likely be strong given the compelling investment case for gold," ANZ said in a note.

In Hong Kong, gold (XAU-HK-PREM) was sold at par to a $2 premium, while in Singapore (XAU-SG-PREM) gold traded at par with the global benchmark to a premium of up to $2.50 per ounce.

In Japan, bullion (XAU-TK-PREM) was sold at a premium of $0.25 to $0.50.

"Purchases from the general public have been strong as prices dropped," said a Tokyo-based trader.

data.title: Etihad Airways Orders 28 Boeing Aircrafts
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Etihad Airways ordered 28 wide-body Boeing aircraft.

The United Arab Emirates airline on Friday said the order includes a mix of Boeing 787 and 777X aircrafts, powered by GE engines and supported by a services package.

The aircrafts are expected to join the fleet from 2028 onward and are part of the airline's long-term strategy to double in size by 2030, it said.

Financial details weren't disclosed.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: Tokyo Century's Profit Jumps 18% in Fiscal 2024
data.description:
Tokyo Century's profit attributable to owners of the parent jumped 18% to 85.3 billion yen for the fiscal year ended March 31, from 72.1 billion yen a year ago.

The financial services company's earnings per share climbed to 173.77 yen from 146.75 yen a year earier, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on Thursday.

Revenue for fiscal 2024 grew 1.7% year on year to 1.369 trillion yen from 1.346 trillion yen a year earlier.

The company declared a year-end dividend of 33.00 yen per share, bringing the full-year dividend to 62.00 yen per share.

For fiscal 2025, the company expects attributable income of 93.0 billion yen and a basic EPS of 190.62 yen.

The company plans to pay total dividend of 68.00 yen per share for the current fiscal year.

data.title: New York Times business news - May 16
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the New York Times business pages. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- The Securities and Exchanges Commission (SEC) is investigating whether Coinbase misstated its user numbers, even after it dropped a lawsuit accusing the crypto exchange of illegally marketing digital currencies to the public.

- Centers for Disease Control and Prevention advisers may limit recommendations for the Covid vaccine, with only older Americans and those with chronic health problems being urged to vaccinate.

- The Trump administration has struck a deal with the United Arab Emirates to deliver hundreds of thousands of advanced Nvidia chips annually to build one of the world’s largest data center hubs.

- A second air traffic control facility in the United States experienced a 90-second communications outage after disruptions raised safety fears and caused flight delays at Newark Liberty Airport near New York.

data.title: Shenghe Resources Acquiring Australia's Peak Rare Earths; Shares Down 4%
data.description:
Shenghe Resources subsidiary Chenguang Rare Earth agreed to fully acquire Australia's Peak Rare Earths for AU$158 million.

The acquisition, including the previously held 19.86% equity, aims to accelerate development of the Ngualla rare earth project in Tanzania, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The deal is subject to regulatory approvals from China, Tanzania and Australia.

Shares of Shenghe Resources were down 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Palm Oil Extends Falls But Heads for Weekly Gains
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures hovered below MYR 3,900 per tonne, marking the second straight decline after data from the Solvent Extractors' Association of India showed palm oil imports in the world's largest buyer fell 24.29% in April to 321,446 tonnes.

Traders also remained cautious ahead of key economic data from China next week, including industrial output, retail sales, and the jobless rate.

Meanwhile, optimism over U.S.-China trade talks faded amid concerns about tariff-related disruptions to global supply chains.

Separately, top producer Indonesia will raise its crude palm oil export levy to 10% from 7.5%, starting May 17 to support a higher biodiesel mandate.

Still, contracts are on track for solid weekly gains, rising over 1% so far and set to break a three-week losing streak, supported by improved export expectations.

Shipments of Malaysian palm oil products for May 1–15 were estimated to have increased between 6.6% and 14.2%, according to cargo surveyors.

data.title: Jingyuan Environmental Protection's Unit to Signs 365 Million Yuan Contract to Build Computing Power Cluster; Shares Down 10%
data.description:
Jiangsu Jingyuan Environmental Protection's unit, Jiangsu Jingyuan Environmental Protection, agreed to construct a computing power cluster for an unnamed client for 364.7 million yuan, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The computing power cluster will include hardware, management software platform, and security equipment and services, the Chinese environmental protection equipment maker said.

Jingyuan Environmental Protection's shares dropped over 10% in recent trade.
